channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A two-pack of DJI’s most capable wireless mics just got its first price cut
item.description: Smartphones these days have incredible cameras that are capable of taking smooth, sharp video, but the microphones are often lacking, to say the least. A wireless lavalier microphone can...

item.title: Bumblebee: Perplexity AI Open-Sources a Safe Supply-Chain Scanner
item.description: Auditing Developer Workstations: Meet Bumblebee When a new software supply-chain vulnerability is announced, security teams face a major challenge: how to quickly check if any developer's local...

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.description: In a surprising blog post on Monday, Microsoft CEO is warning enterprises of the dangers of using proprietary models like Anthropic's and OpenAI's.

item.title: Backend/Domain foundation
item.description: Today I continued my TypeScript learning project after a short break. Instead of jumping into React right away, I decided to keep working on the backend/domain side of the Candidate Tracker...

item.title: 14 Browser Testing Articles That Changed How I Think About Release Confidence
item.description: Modern browser testing is no longer just about clicking a button and checking whether the next page loads. Frontend applications now contain animated route changes, Shadow DOM components, dynamic...

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro are the cheapest they’ve been since January
item.description: Noise-canceling earbuds are great for flights and focusing, but they're not always ideal for outdoor workouts. The last-gen Shokz OpenRun Pro's open-ear design lets you enjoy music while staying...

item.title: How a mesh of peer AI workspaces catches what any single agent misses
item.description: How a mesh of peer AI workspaces catches what any single agent misses Two things about agent fleets get most of the current attention. First: agents fail on stale state, so retrieval and memory...

item.title: The Pixel colors might rule this year
item.description: This year's Google Pixel 11 lineup might come in a bunch of funky colors. A series of now-deleted Amazon listings spotted by 9to5Google show what appear to be placeholders for Google's upcoming...

item.title: Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
item.description: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to...

item.title: Siri AI makes the Apple Watch finally feel like a wrist computer
item.description: Siri has been on the Apple Watch since day one, though I'm usually hard-pressed to find people who actually make good use of it. It's kind of just… been there - mostly as a way to set timers when...

item.title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
item.description: iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to...

item.title: The macOS 27 public beta is worth it just for the Liquid Glass tweaks
item.description: The macOS 27 Golden Gate public beta is here, and anyone with an M-series Mac now has easier access to test-drive Apple's latest changes - including a more subdued Liquid Glass aesthetic. That's...

item.title: Podman in Production: The Book, and the Pipeline Behind It
item.description: My first encounter with Podman back in 2020 was involuntary. A customer with air-gapped RHEL 7 machines that we needed to deploy a containerized microservices stack on. Installing third-party...

item.title: A Practical Workflow for Reusable AI Image Prompts
item.description: Most AI image experiments fail for a boring reason: the prompt is treated as a one-off sentence instead of a reusable specification. A reliable image workflow is closer to a small build system. It...

item.title: Bootcamp Grad Explores Open-Source AI APIs: What I Learned
item.description: Here's the thing: bootcamp Grad Explores Open-Source AI APIs: What I Learned I graduated from a coding bootcamp about six months ago, and honestly, I thought I understood the AI landscape. GPT-4o,...

item.title: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY PRINCIPLE
item.description: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY PRINCIPLE (SRP) A class should have one, and exclusively one, reason to change. In other words, we shouldn't have a single class doing everything: receiving data, validating...

item.title: PRINCÍPIO DA RESPONSABILIDADE ÚNICA
item.description: PRINCÍPIO DA RESPONSABILIDADE ÚNICA Em Inglês: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY PRINCIPLE (SRP) Uma classe deve ter um, e exclusivamente um, motivo para ser alterada. Ou seja, não devemos ter uma única...

item.title: States make last-ditch effort to stop the Paramount ‘media behemoth’
item.description: A dozen state attorneys general are trying to block the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery they warn would raise movie prices and crush cable TV distributors. The states -...

item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring...

item.title: The EV market is on the road to recovery thanks to high gas prices
item.description: As the US war on Iran sent gas prices soaring, American car buyers flocked to electric vehicles - and in particular hybrids. After a sluggish winter and fall, EV sales rebounded in the second...

item.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show

item.title: CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. Unlike other information stealers...

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

item.title: Google and Microsoft Pull ModHeader With 1.6 Million Installs After Dormant Collector Found
item.description: Google and Microsoft have pulled ModHeader, a popular header-editing extension with roughly 1.6 million installs across Chrome and Edge, after researchers found a hidden browsing-history collector...

item.title: The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product...

item.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
item.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: ShareFile Threat, Citrix Bleed 2 Ransomware, AI Coding Attacks, and More
item.description: Somewhere right now, a security tool is quietly finding bugs faster than any human can fix them. That's supposed to be the good news. The catch is that the attackers have the same tools, pointed...

item.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud...
